title: No more dry Sundays in George as council approve liquor sales
description: GEORGE NEWS - George residents will soon be...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - George residents will soon be able to purchase alcohol from liquor outlets on Sundays and religious holidays.

The new by-law was passed in council just after 13:30.


> As soon as the by-law is promulgated in the Government Gazette, alcohol retailers may start sales on these previously “dry” days.


Residents will be able to purchase alcohol from these outlets from 09:00 to 17:00 on Sundays and from 09:00 to 20:00, Mondays to Saturdays.


> The same hours will apply to cellars, wine makers, home distillers and all off-premises consumption retailers.


Shortly before the National Elections, the item was removed from a council meeting agenda, for reasons unknown.


> Huge majority


At today’s meeting, not everyone voted for the item, with the ACDP abstaining. However, the vast majority of councillors approved the by-law and the item carried with 44 votes for and 3 votes against – all from the Patriotic Alliance.

In total, four councillors decided not to vote.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
